topic: A person finds their spouse's secret savings account
raw_output:
[story 1]

The envelope was thick, the paper feeling like a lead weight in my hand. Inside, a bank statement, stark figures detailing a life I hadn't known existed. My breath hitched, a shallow, quick intake of air that burned in my chest. I traced the numbers with a trembling finger, each digit a small, sharp shard of realization. Looking up, I saw Elias across the room, engrossed in his phone, a picture of oblivious contentment. The silence in the apartment amplified the frantic drumming in my ears. Every movement felt magnified, every second stretching into an eternity as the weight of this hidden reality settled over me. I couldn't bring myself to move, the urge to either scream or flee warring violently within my core.

[story 2]

It was tucked inside a seldom-used book, a slip of paper that smelled faintly of old paper and something metallic. When I unfolded it, the figures swam before my eyes. They represented a safety net, a secret reservoir of funds that existed entirely outside the shared narrative we’d built. A cold sweat broke out along my spine, prickling my skin. I dropped the paper, the sound barely audible, and immediately began pacing the length of the living room. My hands clenched into fists so tight my knuckles turned white. I kept glancing toward the bedroom door, a silent plea for an escape route that didn't materialize. The air in the room felt suddenly too thin, suffocating.

[story 3]

The discovery happened during a routine inventory of shared documents. A small, unassuming folder held the key—a ledger detailing accounts I had never been privy to. My vision tunneled slightly. The sheer scale of the discrepancy made my stomach revolt. I sat down heavily on the edge of the sofa, my back pressing against the cool upholstery, trying to anchor myself against the sudden, dizzying instability. I stared at the page, my gaze refusing to leave the columns of numbers. A slow, deliberate exhale escaped me, ragged and uneven. The world outside the frame of the document seemed distant, muted, as if I were submerged underwater.
